description.en: Although the `groupadd` program will not let you create a duplicate Group ID (GID), it is possible for an administrator to manually edit the `/etc/group` file and change the GID field.

rationale.en: User groups must be assigned unique GIDs for accountability and to ensure appropriate access protections.

audit.en: Based on the results of the audit script, establish unique GIDs and review all files owned by the shared GID to determine which group they are supposed to belong to.

remediation.en: Based on the results of the audit script, establish unique GIDs and review all files owned by the shared GID to determine which group they are supposed to belong to.

references: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: CM-1, CM-2, CM-6, CM-7, IA-5
